bead.title: align routing score with cheapest-qualified policy
bead.description:
<context-digest>Specs amended under fizeau-f0a71a4c. FEAT-004/ADR-011 define cheapest-qualified ranking: after hard gates, default inclusion, metered opt-in, capability, quota, and auto-routability, Fizeau should prefer the lowest effective marginal cost candidate whose soft power fit is sufficient. Power bounds remain soft scoring inputs, and exact model pins retain identity. Frontier subscription bonuses must not make power>=9 routes too attractive under default max_power=8.</context-digest>
Adjust score components so bounded default routing does not over-prefer frontier subscription routes and tests prove free-but-underpowered routes do not beat in-band routine candidates solely on cost.
bead.acceptance: Healthy power>=9 subscription bonus does not apply when effective MaxPower is below 9; default policy prefers in-band qualified candidates over materially underpowered free candidates; smart policy still allows frontier subscription/cloud routes when healthy and allowed; power scoring tests document soft undershoot/overshoot behavior
